question.label: Done means what
question.name: What does 'done' mean to you on the tickets you've picked up so far?
question.description: Checks whether his mental model of completion matches the handoff expectations.

answer: I usually wait too long before asking because I want to solve things myself first.

assessment.note: He describes delaying asks because he is trying to self-solve first, which signals an unclear threshold for when to seek help.

new_queue.label: Thread follow
new_queue.name: usually wait long — can you say more about what that means for you right now?

new_queue.label: Ask threshold
new_queue.name: What tells you it's time to ask instead of keep pushing alone?

new_queue.label: Ship-time hesitation
new_queue.name: What tends to make shipping feel risky right before you send it?

new_queue.label: Tooling friction
new_queue.name: What part of your workflow is slowing you down most right now?

new_queue.label: Go-to people
new_queue.name: Who do you already feel comfortable going to when you get stuck?

new_queue.label: Next-step focus
new_queue.name: Given what we've covered, what would you want to tighten up first?

issues: coverage: injected engagement into next question (0 touches after turn 4)

history.answer_excerpt: I can do the tasks, but I still second-guess whether my approach matches team expectations.
I can do the tasks, but I still second-guess whether my approach matches team expectations.
Local setup is better now, but I still lose time when the design and API details do not match.
I am not always sure who should approve what before I push.
